hints.title: Hint 1
hints.content: Consider for a given $$$p$$$, which elements can be placed at $$$p$$$ with one reversal operation.

solutions.title: Tutorial
solutions.content:
2222B - Artistic Balance Tree
Note that when reversing a segment of odd length, the parity of the indices remains unchanged. It can be shown that through a single reversal operation, we can move any element from a position with the same parity as $$$x_i$$$ to the target position $$$x_i$$$.
Using a greedy approach, we aim to mark the largest numbers first and avoid marking negative numbers when possible. Our strategy is: – If the maximum value in the group of positions with the same parity as $$$x_i$$$ is greater than $$$0$$$, or if no marks have been made in that group yet, we mark that maximum value. – Otherwise, we mark the largest value from any previously marked position in the same group.
We can solve this by separating the elements by parity and applying any sorting algorithm to process the groups.
